intended_use: Brainomix Triage LVO is a notification-only, parallel workflow tool for use by hospital networks and trained clinicians to identify and communicate images of specific patients to a specialist, indepen
intended_use_full: Brainomix Triage LVO is a notification-only, parallel workflow tool for use by hospital networks and trained clinicians to identify and communicate images of specific patients to a specialist, independent of standard of care workflow. Brainomix Triage LVO uses an artificial intelligence algorithm to analyze images for findings suggestive of a prespecified clinical condition and to notify an appropriate medical specialist of these findings in parallel to standard of care image interpretation. Identification of suspected findings is not for diagnostic use beyond notification. Specifically, the device analyzes CT angiogram images of the brain acquired in the acute setting, and sends notifications to a neurovascular specialist that a suspected large vessel occlusion (LVO) has been identified and recommends review of those images. Brainomix Triage LVO is intended to analyze terminal ICA and MCA-M1 vessels for LVOs.
target_condition: Large vessel occlusion (LVO) of the intracranial internal carotid artery (ICA) and proximal middle cerebral artery (M1 segment)
ground_truth: Two ABR-certified (US board-certified) neuroradiologists reviewed each case independently; consensus determined by a third neuroradiologist in event of disagreement
other_metrics: Time-to-notification ranged from 86.3 to 178.2 seconds, meeting goal of ≤3.5 minutes. Left hemisphere LVO sensitivity 88.6% (CI: 79.4-94.8%), right hemisphere 91.4% (CI: 83.0-96.7%). ICA-specific sensitivity 96.6% (CI: 84.3-99.8%), MCA M1 sensitivity 88.7% (CI: 81.3-93.5%). Performance stratified by age, gender, race, scanner manufacturer, clinical site.
